---
title: "Stablecoin Market Cap Visualization"
format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    page-layout: full
    theme: cosmo
jupyter: python3
---

{{< include _dashboard_content.qmd >}}

### 📈 Interactive Charts

::: {layout-ncol=2}

```{python}
#| title: Market Cap History
import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px

# Load data
df = pd.read_csv("data/stablecoin_marketcap.csv")

# Ensure date is datetime
df["date"] = pd.to_datetime(df["date"], errors="coerce")

# Line Plot
fig = px.line(df, x="date", y="market_cap", color="coin_name", title="Stablecoin Market Cap Over Time")
fig.show()
```

```{python}
#| title: Market Dominance
# Get latest date
latest_date = df['date'].max()
latest_df = df[df['date'] == latest_date].copy()

# Sort by market cap descending
latest_df = latest_df.sort_values(by='market_cap', ascending=False)

# Pie Chart
fig_pie = px.pie(latest_df, values='market_cap', names='coin_name', title=f'Market Dominance ({latest_date.strftime("%Y-%m-%d")})')
fig_pie.show()
```


:::

### 🗞️ News Archive

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
import pandas as pd
from IPython.display import Markdown
import os

archive_path = 'data/news_archive.csv'

if os.path.exists(archive_path):
    df_news = pd.read_csv(archive_path)
    
    # Process if data exists
    if not df_news.empty:
         # Ensure datetime
        df_news['published_dt'] = pd.to_datetime(df_news['published_dt'], errors='coerce')
        df_news = df_news.sort_values(by='published_dt', ascending=False)
        
        import html

        # Create clickable title
        # Security: Sanitize title and link to prevent Markdown injection, XSS, and Quarto shortcode injection
        def create_safe_link(title, link):
            safe_title = html.escape(str(title)).replace('[', '&#91;').replace(']', '&#93;')
            safe_title = safe_title.replace('{', '&#123;').replace('}', '&#125;')
            safe_title = safe_title.replace('\n', ' ').replace('\r', '').replace('|', '&#124;')
            safe_link = str(link) if str(link).startswith(('http://', 'https://')) else '#'
            safe_link = safe_link.replace('{', '%7B').replace('}', '%7D')
            safe_link = safe_link.replace(' ', '%20').replace('(', '%28').replace(')', '%29').replace('"', '%22')
            safe_link = safe_link.replace('\n', '').replace('\r', '').replace('|', '%7C')
            return f"[{safe_title}]({safe_link})"

        df_news['Article'] = df_news.apply(lambda x: create_safe_link(x['title'], x['link']), axis=1)
        
        # Security: Sanitize published date as well
        df_news['published'] = df_news['published'].apply(lambda x: html.escape(str(x)).replace('[', '&#91;').replace(']', '&#93;').replace('{', '&#123;').replace('}', '&#125;').replace('\n', ' ').replace('\r', '').replace('|', '&#124;'))

        # Select and Rename columns
        df_table = df_news[['published', 'Article']].copy()
        df_table.columns = ['Published Date', 'Article']
        
        # Display as Markdown Table
        print(df_table.to_markdown(index=False))
    else:
        print("No archived news available.")
else:
    print("News archive file not found.")
```
